recalls.Description:
JJGoo has issued a recall for its LED balloon lights due to safety concerns. These submersible lights, sold in packs of 100, feature color-changing LEDs and contain 200 preinstalled LR41 batteries. Each light is approximately 0.6 inches in diameter and is labeled with "MY1005E-Colorfu1-100" on the packaging. The design allows easy access to the batteries, posing a significant ingestion risk to children.

The recalled lights do not meet the safety requirements outlined by Reese's Law, which mandates specific warnings for products containing button cell batteries. Swallowing these batteries can lead to severe injuries, including internal chemical burns and even death. It is crucial for consumers to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Recall of JJGoo LED Balloon Lights Due to Serious Injury Risk from Battery Ingestion
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact JJGoo via email at JJGooLEDBalloonLightsrecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled JJGoo LED Balloon Lights
The recalled JJGoo balloon lights were sold in packs of 100 LED color-changing lights
Each of the multi-color recalled JJGoo balloon lights has 200 preinstalled LR41 batteries
"MY1005E-Colorfu1-100" is printed on a label on the product packaging of the recalled JJGoo LED balloon lights

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from May 2025 through November 2025 for about $23.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Yimu Technology Co., Ltd. dba JJGoo, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible button cell batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lights, keep them away from children, and dispose of batteries properly; contact JJGoo for a full refund.

seo.metaDescription: JJGoo recalls LED balloon lights due to ingestion risks from accessible batteries. Immediate action required for consumer safety.
seo.ogTitle: Recall of JJGoo LED Balloon Lights Due to Serious Injury Risk from Battery Ingestion
seo.ogDescription: JJGoo recalls LED balloon lights due to ingestion risks from accessible batteries. Immediate action required for consumer safety.

seo.twitterDescription: JJGoo recalls LED balloon lights due to ingestion risks from accessible batteries. Immediate action required for consumer safety.

recalls.Description: Dresbe is recalling its LED Christmas Headbands due to a serious safety hazard. These novelty headbands, sold in a two-pack featuring a red candy cane design and a green Christmas tree design, contain three button cell batteries that can be easily accessed by children. This poses a significant risk of ingestion, which can lead to severe injuries or even death. The product packaging is labeled with "HB-052" and "DRESBE LED Christmas Headband (2pc)."
recalls.Title: Dresbe LED Christmas Headbands Recalled Due to Battery Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Dresbe via email at dresberecall@shineprom.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Dresbe Christmas Headbands (with candy cane and Christmas tree attachments)
Recalled Dresbe Christmas Headbands (with lights)
Label affixed to the recalled headband package

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com from March 2024 through June 2025 for about $13.

Distributors.Name: Wenxi Xuefeng Technology Co., Ltd, dba Dresbe, of China

Hazards.Name: The headbands present a risk of ingestion due to easily accessible button cell batteries, violating safety standards and lacking necessary warnings.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the headbands and contact Dresbe for disposal instructions. A photo of the disposal must be emailed to dresberecall@shineprom.com to process a refund. Note: Dispose of button cell batteries according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

seo.metaDescription: Dresbe recalls LED Christmas Headbands due to battery ingestion risks. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.
seo.ogTitle: Dresbe LED Christmas Headbands Recalled Due to Battery Ingestion Risk
seo.ogDescription: Dresbe recalls LED Christmas Headbands due to battery ingestion risks. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.

seo.twitterDescription: Dresbe recalls LED Christmas Headbands due to battery ingestion risks. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.

recalls.Description: Kitosun is recalling its Submersible LED Lights due to a serious risk of battery ingestion that could lead to severe injury or death. These lights feature 10 color-changing LEDs and come with two remote controls, containing a total of 20 preinstalled CR2450 lithium batteries in the lights and four CR2032 lithium batteries in the remotes. Each light measures approximately 3.5 cm in diameter. It is important to note that button cell and coin batteries are dangerous, and consumers should dispose of them following local hazardous waste guidelines.
recalls.Title: Kitosun Submersible LED Lights Recalled Due to Battery Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Kitosun via email at sales@kitosun.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Kitosun Submersible LED Lights
Recalled Kitosun Submersible LED Lights

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from March 2024 through November 2025 for about $23.

Distributors.Name: Kitosun Original Company Limited, dba Kitosun, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard from accessible lithium coin batteries, violating safety standards for consumer products.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the lights out of children's reach and contact Kitosun for a full refund or a replacement. A photo of the product in the trash will be required to process the refund or replacement, which can be emailed to gumilius@outlook.com.

seo.metaDescription: Kitosun recalls Submersible LED Lights due to battery ingestion risks. Contact for refunds or replacements.
seo.ogTitle: Kitosun Submersible LED Lights Recalled Due to Battery Ingestion Hazard
seo.ogDescription: Kitosun recalls Submersible LED Lights due to battery ingestion risks. Contact for refunds or replacements.

seo.twitterDescription: Kitosun recalls Submersible LED Lights due to battery ingestion risks. Contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: Energizer is recalling its Large Power Indicator Lantern due to a potential overheating issue. This portable lantern features a cone shape with an opaque lens and prominently displays the "Energizer" brand. It has a gray top with a large power button and a red handle. The affected model number is BGAAL9, and the SKU numbers are 039800143341 and 039800143334. Only lanterns with a date code of June 2024, indicated on the base of the unit, are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: Energizer Recalls Large Power Indicator Lanterns Due to Overheating Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Energizer at 800-383-7323, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email customersupport@energizer.com or visit https://energizer.com/lightings/lanternrecall or www.energizer.com and select the "Lantern Recall" tab.

Images.Caption: Recalled Energizer® Large Power Indicator Lantern, model BGAAL9
Date code located on the base of recalled lantern

Injuries.Name: The firm reported one incident of the lithium-ion battery in a lantern overheating. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Meijer, Blain, and Fleet Wholesale stores nationwide and online at Amazon.com between January and November 2025 for between $35 to $45. In addition, approximately 100 lanterns were distributed to reviewers through the Bazaarvoice marketing program.

Importers.Name: Energizer Holdings, Inc., of Clayton, Missouri

Hazards.Name: Overheating of lithium-ion batteries posing burn risks

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lantern and contact Energizer for a full refund

seo.metaDescription: Energizer recalls Large Power Indicator Lanterns due to overheating risks. Stop using immediately and contact for a refund.

recalls.Description: The Isla Rae brand magnetic wireless chargers have been recalled due to potential fire and burn hazards. These chargers, which attach magnetically to the back of compatible phones, were sold in white, pink, and purple colors. The model number 'RM5PBM' is located on the side of the charger, beneath the label '5000 mAh 3.7V.' It is crucial to handle the disposal of these lithium-ion batteries properly to prevent fire risks, as they cannot be discarded in regular trash or recycling bins. Instead, check with your local household hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Chargers Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks; Available at T.J. Maxx and Marshalls
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact TJX at 888-256-1564 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email powerbank@realtimeresults.net, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/powerbank. For Marshalls, visit https://m.marshalls.com/us/m/jump/topic/Product-Recalls/2400019 or Marshalls.com and scroll to the bottom for 'Product Recalls.' For T.J. Maxx, go to https://m.tjmaxx.tjx.com/m/jump/topic/product-recalls/2400019 or TJmaxx.com and check the bottom of the page for 'Product Recalls.'

Products.Name: Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 13,200 (In addition, about 7,000 units were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger (white)
Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger (pink)
Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger (purple)
Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger Model: RM5PBM

Injuries.Name: None reported.

Retailers.Name: Marshalls and T.J. Maxx stores nationwide between June 2024 and November 2025 for $15.

Importers.Name: Hello to Green dba Press Play Products of Bell, California

Distributors.Name: The TJX Companies Inc., of Framingham, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Explosion risk during use leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the chargers immediately and visit https://www.recallrtr.com/powerbank to register for the recall and receive a full refund. After registration, dispose of the charger according to local regulations and do not throw it in the household trash.

recalls.Description: INIU is recalling its 10,000mAh portable power banks, specifically model BI-B41, due to potential fire and burn hazards. These power banks come in black or blue and feature the INIU logo along with a paw-print LED light on the front. Only those units with serial numbers 000G21, 000H21, 000I21, and 000L21 are affected by this recall. It is crucial to handle the disposal of these lithium-ion batteries properly, as they pose a higher fire risk compared to standard batteries. Do not dispose of them in regular trash or recycling bins. Instead, check with your local municipal household hazardous waste (HHW) center for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: INIU Recalls BI-B41 Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks; Available on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach INIU at 888-886-3606 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@iniu.shop, or visit https://iniushop.com/pages/recall-b41# for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled INIU Power Bank – model BI-B41

Injuries.Name: INIU has received 15 reports of power banks overheating, including 11 reports of fires that resulted in three minor burn injuries and property damage totaling over $380,000.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Topstar Industry Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from August 2021 and April 2022 for about $18.

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition from lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Proper disposal guidance for recalled power banks

seo.metaDescription: INIU recalls BI-B41 power banks due to fire hazards. Learn how to dispose of them safely and get consumer support.

recalls.Description: KTEBO has issued a recall for its Writing Tablet Toys due to a serious safety hazard. These toys, sold in pairs and available in four color combinations (pink/blue, green/yellow, orange/blue, purple/red), come in two sizes: 8.5 inches and 10 inches, each accompanied by a stylus. The toys fail to meet safety standards due to a faulty battery compartment screw, which can pose a risk of battery ingestion. Ingested button cell batteries can lead to severe injuries, internal burns, or even death.
recalls.Title: KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys Recalled Due to Battery Ingestion Risk; Sold on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to KTEBO via email at writing-tablet-recall@ktebo.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys in Blue/Pink (front)
Recalled KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys in Blue/Pink (back)
Recalled KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys in Yellow/Green (front)
Recalled KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys in Green/Yellow (back)
Recalled KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys in Blue/Orange (front)
Recalled KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys in Blue/Orange (back)
Recalled KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys in Red/Purple (front)
Recalled KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys in Purple/Red (back)
Recalled KTEBO Writing Tablet Toys Packaging

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from September 2025 through October 2025 for between $8 and $30.

Distributors.Name: Banghe Guangzhou International Trade Co., Ltd., dba KTEBO, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion due to inadequate battery compartment security.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys immediately and contact KTEBO for a free replacement. Mark the recalled toys with 'RECALLED', photograph them, and email the images to writing-tablet-recall@ktebo.com with 'Recall Proof' in the subject line. Properly dispose of button cell batteries following local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.Description: Belkin is recalling specific models of portable power banks and wireless charging stands due to potential fire and burn hazards. The affected charging stands are identified by model number MMA008, while the power banks include models BPB002 and PB0003. Serial numbers for the charging stands begin with '57X' and are located on the bottom, while the power banks have serial numbers starting with '35S' printed on the back. Both the charging stands and BPB002 power banks were sold in black, and the PB0003 model was available in black or white.
recalls.Title: Belkin Recalls Portable Power Banks and Wireless Charging Stands Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Belkin at 800-223-5546 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit http://www.belkin.com/MMA008recall and http://www.belkin.com/BPB002recall for more information. Additional details can be found on the Belkin website under 'Product Recalls.'

Products.Name: Wireless Charging Stand MMA008
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 83,500 (In addition, about 2,385 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled MMA008 Portable Wireless Charging Stand
Location of model number on the recalled MMA008 Portable Wireless Charging Stand (in red box)
Recalled BPB002 Portable Power Bank
Location of model number on the recalled BPB002 Portable Power Bank (in red box)
Recalled PB003 Portable Power Bank

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a fire incident in the U.S. (and 15 reports internationally). Two minor burn injuries and property damage totaling $37,765 have been reported outside of the U.S.

Manufacturers.Name: Belkin International Inc., of El Segundo, California

Retailers.Name: Best Buy, Target, Walmart and other stores nationwide and online at Belkin.com, Amazon.com and on Tiktok from August 2020 through August 2025 for between $29 and $180.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled products and contact Belkin for a full refund or store credit. Submit photos of the product with your recall form. Refunds are available with or without a receipt, and proper disposal instructions for lithium-ion batteries must be followed.

recalls.Description: Great Lakes Wholesale International is recalling specific battery packs due to safety concerns related to battery ingestion. The affected products are labeled with "Great Lakes Select" on the back and include various item numbers and battery types. These packs pose a risk as they lack child-resistant packaging and necessary warning labels, making them dangerous for children. Ingesting these batteries can lead to severe injuries, including internal burns and even fatalities.
recalls.Title: Great Lakes Wholesale International Recalls Select Battery Packs Due to Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Great Lakes Select Battery Packs and keep them out of children's reach. For more information and to request a full refund, contact Great Lakes Wholesale International at 708-597-6000 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, or email Recall@Glwholesale.com. Additional details can be found at www.glwholesale.com under the "Recall" section.

Products.Name: Great Lakes Select Battery Pack CR2032

Recalled Mixed Button Cell Batteries, Item 00271
Recalled Mixed Super Alkaline Button Cell Batteries, Item 00272
Recalled LR44 Button Cell Batteries, Item 00276

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Downeast Wholesalers Renys, International Wholesale, Lots & More, Northwoods Wholesale Outlet, a Ben Franklin store in Tennessee, and other stores nationwide from September 2024 through August 2025 for between $0.65 and $2.25.

Distributors.Name: Great Lakes Wholesale International of Lockport, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injuries from battery ingestion due to lack of child-resistant packaging and warning labels.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the battery packs, keep them away from children, and contact Great Lakes Wholesale International for a refund. Dispose of batteries following local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.Description:
The recall now includes additional ESR HaloLock wireless power banks, specifically model 2G505, alongside previously recalled models 2G520, 2G505B, and 2G512B. These pocket-sized power banks feature the brand name "ESR" on the back and model numbers on the right side. Model 2G505 is available in black and white, while the other models come in various colors including dark blue, light blue, gray, white, and black. All recalled units have five circular LED lights on one side.

Consumers should be aware that the lithium-ion batteries in these power banks can overheat and pose serious fire and burn risks. Immediate action is necessary for safety.
recalls.Title: Expanded Recall of ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Waymeet at 888-990-0280 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email support@esrtech.com, or visit https://www.esrtech.com/pages/recall or www.esrtech.com and select "Product Recalls" at the top of the page.

Products.Name: ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Bank Model 2G505
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 19,500 (In addition, about 2,015 were sold in Canada). Waymeet previously recalled 24,000 power banks on August 14, 2025 (In addition, 9,900 were sold in Canada that were covered in the August recall)

Images.Caption: Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G505
Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G520
Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G505B
Recalled ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Bank Model 2G512B

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 20 reports of the lithium-ion battery catching fire and exploding, resulting in property damage of about $30,000. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com (all models), Esrtech.com (all models) and Homedepot.com (models 2G520, 2G505B and 2G512B only), from September 2022 through July 2025 for between $32 and $50.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact Waymeet for a full refund. Send a photo of the recalled power bank with the model number marked as "Recalled" to support@esrtech.com. Dispose of the power banks according to local hazardous waste regulations.

seo.metaDescription: Important recall notice for ESR HaloLock power banks due to fire hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Zyntony has announced a recall of Kogalla power banks, specifically models BP125, BatPak 2F, and BatPak 3F. These rechargeable USB power banks, equipped with lithium-ion batteries of 6,700mAh, 13,400mAh, or 20,100mAh capacities, were sold individually and as part of Kogalla trail light packages. The Kogalla logo is visible on the front, while the model number can be found on the back. Notably, the BP125 model features a black USB-A jack, whereas the BatPak 2F and 3F models have orange USB-A jacks. It is crucial not to dispose of these recalled lithium-ion batteries in regular trash or recycling bins due to their fire risk. Instead, consumers should check with their local hazardous waste collection centers for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: Zyntony Issues Recall for Kogalla Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Kogalla via email at bpreplace@kogalla.com, or visit https://rtn.kogalla.com/pages/kogalla-recalls-batpak1-batpak-2f-batpak-3f or https://kogalla.com/ and click on “Recall Information” for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Kogalla rechargeable USB power banks - front
Recalled Kogalla rechargeable USB power banks - back

Injuries.Name: Zyntony has received two reports of lithium-ion battery overheating and catching fire, even when not in use, resulting in one minor burn injury and property damage, totaling about $3,300.

Retailers.Name: Available on Kogalla.com from June 2024 to January 2025 for the BP125 and BatPak 2F models, as well as the trail lights sold with them; and from August 2024 to January 2025 for the BatPak 3F model and its associated trail lights. Prices ranged from $45 to $90 for the power banks and from $180 to $230 for the trail lights with power banks.

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition of lithium-ion batteries, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled power banks and contact Kogalla for a free replacement, including shipping. An online form at https://rtn.kogalla.com/products/request-batpak-replacement must be completed. Proper disposal of the recalled power banks is also required per local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Zyntony recalls Kogalla power banks due to fire hazards. Immediate action is required for consumers. Learn more about the recall and replacement process.

recalls.Description: The Arizer Solo II, a portable vaporizer powered by a lithium-ion battery, is being recalled due to potential fire and burn hazards. This device features a sturdy anodized aluminum body and comes in Carbon Black and Mystic Blue. It has a digital display for temperature settings and a stainless steel heating chamber. Only units with serial numbers beginning with 'M2' are affected, which can be found etched on the bottom of the device. The product packaging includes UPC 628078800836.
recalls.Title: Recall of Arizer Solo II Vaporizers Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the Arizer Solo II immediately and reach out to 7111495 Canada at 888-291-0521 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email recall@arizer.com or visit https://arizer.com/solo-ii-voluntary-recall-submission-form/ or www.arizer.ca and click on 'Product Recalls' for further details.

Products.Name: Arizer Solo II Portable Vaporizer
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 5,460 (in addition, about 2,820 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Arizer Solo II portable electronic vaporizer devices in Blue and Black

Injuries.Name: The firm has received nine reports of the battery overheating, producing smoke, or ejecting material. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at specialty, health and wellness, and adult novelty stores across the country, as well as on arizer.com from June 2024 through July 2025 for approximately $170.

Importers.Name: 111495 Canada Inc., dba Arizer Tech, of Waterloo, Ontario

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating, smoke production, and material ejection from the lithium-ion battery, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the vaporizer and contact 7111495 Canada for a complimentary replacement. Affected customers will receive a Solo II MAX and wax for safe disposal. The recalled unit must be disabled with the provided wax in the charging port and disposed of at a hazardous waste facility. Do not discard this lithium-ion battery or device in regular trash or recycling bins. Check with local hazardous waste centers for proper disposal options.

recalls.metaDescription: Arizer recalls Solo II vaporizers due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: CT-ENERGY is recalling its Lithium Coin Battery Chargers, specifically model "nc-02", which come with four rechargeable 2032 lithium-ion batteries and a USB cable. The product is identifiable by the brand name "CT-ENERGY" on the front and the model number printed on the back. The design poses significant safety hazards, particularly for children, due to easy access to the lithium coin batteries included with the charger. These batteries are not packaged in a child-resistant manner, violating safety standards.
recalls.Title: CT-ENERGY Lithium Coin Battery Chargers Recalled Over Serious Ingestion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to CT New Energy Technology via email at info@ct-energy.cn or visit www.ct-energy.cn/about/22.html?land=en-us. For more details, click on "Urgent Product Safety Recall Notice" on their homepage.

Products.Name: CT-ENERGY Lithium Coin Battery Charger Set

Images.Caption: Recalled CT-ENERGY Lithium Coin Battery Charger with Rechargeable 2032 Batteries

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com and Walmart.com from March 2024 through May 2025 for approximately $17.

Distributors.Name: CT (Foshan) New Energy Technology Co., Ltd. (dba CT-Energy), of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion due to non-child-resistant packaging and accessibility of batteries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the charger, remove batteries, and contact CT New Energy Technology for a full refund, Child-Resistant Safety Case, or replacement kit.

recalls.metaDescription: CT-ENERGY recalls Lithium Coin Battery Chargers due to serious ingestion risks. Contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: ESR HaloLock wireless power banks, identified by model numbers 2G520, 2G505B, and 2G512B, are being recalled due to potential fire hazards. These compact power banks feature the brand name 'ESR' on the back and the model number on the right side. They are available in various colors including dark blue, light blue, gray, white, and black, and all models include five circular LED display lights on one side.
recalls.Title: Recall of ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately cease using the affected power banks and reach out to Waymeet at 888-990-0280 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday. They can also email support@esrtech.com or visit https://www.esrtech.com/pages/recall for more information.

Products.Name: ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Banks
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 24,000 (In addition, about 9,900 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G520
Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G505B
Recalled ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Bank Model 2G512B

Injuries.Name: The firm has received nine reports of the lithium-ion battery catching fire and exploding, resulting in property damage of about $20,000. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com, Homedepot.com, and Esrtech.com from September 2023 to July 2025, priced between $32 and $40.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards from overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the power banks and contact Waymeet for a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: ESR HaloLock power banks recalled due to fire risks. Stop using immediately and contact Waymeet for a refund.

recalls.Description: The recall affects the iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank, specifically the 5000 mAh model number IST-09991/W05. These power banks feature the iStore logo on the front and the model number on the back. They are designed to work with multiple mobile devices, measuring approximately 3 inches by 2 inches by 0.25 inches and weighing around 3.2 ounces. Each unit was sold with a USB-C to USB-C cable that is about 12 inches long. It is crucial to dispose of these recalled lithium-ion batteries properly to prevent fire hazards. Do not discard them in regular trash or recycling bins. Check with your local household hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: Recall of iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks; Imported by Tomauri
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact iStore at 800-268-4049 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at recall@istore.co, or visit https://istore.co/pages/battery-recall and https://www.tomauri.com/pages/battery-recall. You can also go to http://www.tomauri.com and click on "Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (Model IST-09991/W05)
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 8,520 (An additional 6,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (packaging)
Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (front)
Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (back)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the wireless power banks overheating and exploding while they were charging, including one incident that resulted in a minor burn to the victim's arm. The firm received reports of property damage of about $15,000.

Retailers.Name: Available at vending machines and stores in various airports nationwide, as well as online at iStore.co from February 2023 through May 2025, priced between $46 and $65.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power bank immediately and visit the firm's website to participate in the recall. You will need to submit two photos via email: one showing the front of the power bank marked with "Recalled" and the date, and the other displaying the model number on the back. A purchase receipt is not mandatory but may be requested. Dispose of the power bank according to local hazardous waste regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Banks due to fire hazards. Immediate action required for safe disposal.

recalls.Description: DR Power has announced a recall of its 62-volt 5.0 Ah LiPRO rechargeable lithium-ion battery packs, which are designed for use with various DR Power battery-operated products, including lawnmowers, trimmers, and snow throwers. The recall affects stand-alone battery packs with the model number 414240, as well as removable battery packs that come with outdoor equipment models 524340, SB11022XEN, T4X3026XEN, T4X3062XENR, CE75021XEN0, and CE77021XEN0. Consumers can identify the recalled battery packs by the model number printed on the label. It is crucial to handle these batteries properly to avoid fire hazards, as they should not be disposed of in regular trash or recycling bins.
recalls.Title: DR Power Recalls Lithium-Ion Battery Packs Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact DR Power at 800-454-8643 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@drpower.com. Additional details can be found online at https://drpower.com/recalls and https://www.drpower.com/ under "Product Recalls."

Products.Name: DR Power LiPRO 5.0 Ah Lithium-Ion Battery Pack

DR Power Model 524340, DR Trimmer Mower
DR Power Model SB11022XEN, DR Pulse 62V Snow Thrower

DR Power Model CE75021XEN0, DR Pulse 62V Mower 21"

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of incidents of fire and/or overheating. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Ningbo New Team Import & Export Co. Ltd. ("Lera"), of China

Retailers.Name: Available at home improvement and hardware stores nationwide, as well as online at DRPower.com and CountryHomeProducts.com from April 2018 through July 2024, priced between $300 and $700 for outdoor equipment including battery packs, and $250 for individual battery packs.

Importers.Name: Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to potential short-circuiting and ignition

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled battery packs immediately and contact DR Power for a pro-rated refund. Proof of destruction is required by submitting two photos as per the provided instructions on the recall site, along with the online recall form at https://www.drpower.com/5AHB-recall. Dispose of the battery packs according to local regulations if not returning.

recalls.metaDescription: DR Power recalls lithium-ion battery packs due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a refund and dispose of them safely.

recalls.Description:
Anker Innovations has issued a recall for their PowerCore 10000 power banks, specifically model number A1263, due to potential fire and burn hazards. The affected units have the brand name 'Anker' engraved on the front, with the model number A1263 and serial number printed on the bottom. Only units sold in the U.S. with specific serial numbers are included in this recall. Consumers can verify if their device is affected by visiting https://www.anker.com/product-recalls.

It is crucial not to dispose of recalled lithium-ion batteries or devices in regular trash or recycling bins, as they pose a significant fire risk. Instead, check with your local household hazardous waste (HHW) collection center for proper disposal options. Before visiting, confirm that they accept recalled lithium-ion batteries to ensure safe disposal.
recalls.Title: Over One Million Anker PowerCore 10000 Power Banks Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Anker at 800-988-7973 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, or via email at support@anker.com. More information is available at https://www.anker.com/product-recalls, or through the Live Chat option at https://support.anker.com/s/contact-us.

Images.Caption: Recalled Anker A1263 power bank

Injuries.Name: Anker has received 19 reports of fires and explosions. This includes two reports of minor burn injuries not requiring medical attention and 11 reports of property damage totaling over $60,700.

Retailers.Name: Available online through Anker, Amazon, Newegg, and eBay from June 2016 to December 2022, priced around $27.

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact Anker for a free replacement. Registration for the recall can be completed at https://www.anker.com/product-recalls.

recalls.metaDescription: Anker recalls over one million PowerCore 10000 power banks due to fire and burn hazards. Check if your device is affected.

recalls.Description:
The iWALK Magnetic Wireless Power Banks have been recalled due to potential fire and burn hazards associated with their lithium-ion batteries. Each power bank is marked with "iWALK" on the front, while model and serial numbers can be found on the back. These compact power banks, available in various colors, feature a circular LED display that indicates remaining battery life. Specific models affected include DBL6000M and DBL6000P, which also have a circular phone holder ring above the LED display. A comprehensive list of model numbers and serial numbers is included in the recall notice. Consumers are advised to handle these devices with caution and follow proper disposal guidelines to mitigate risks. 

It is critical not to dispose of these recalled batteries in regular trash or recycling streams due to their fire risk. Instead, check with your local hazardous waste collection center to see if they accept these items. Always contact the center beforehand to confirm their policies regarding recalled lithium-ion batteries.
recalls.Title: Recall of iWALK Magnetic Wireless Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to U2O Global Co. Ltd. at their toll-free number 888-279-0111, available from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. You can also email them at service@iwalk.net or visit https://www.iwalk.net/recall.html for more information.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 15 reports of the lithium-ion battery catching fire, resulting in 10 burn injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: U2O Global Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at iWalkMall.com, Amazon.com, and other e-commerce platforms from July 2021 through March 2024, priced between $20 and $57.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact U2O Global for a full refund. To claim your refund, email a photo of the power bank showing the model and serial number, with "Recalled" written on it, to service@iwalk.net. Dispose of the power banks following local hazardous waste disposal guidelines.

recalls.metaDescription: iWALK Magnetic Wireless Power Banks recalled due to fire hazards. Stop using and contact U2O Global for a refund and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: Baseus is recalling its 65W 30000mAh portable chargers, identified by model number BS-30KP365, due to a fire hazard linked to overheating lithium-ion batteries. These chargers, available in black and white, feature '65w' on the front and have a label on the bottom that includes the brand name, model number, and a serial number starting with 'SN' and ending in a digit from 0 to 9 or the letter 'D.' It is crucial not to dispose of these chargers in regular trash or recycling bins as they pose a significant fire risk. Instead, contact your local hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal guidance.
recalls.Title: Baseus 65W Portable Chargers Recalled Due to Fire Risk; Available on Amazon and Other Retailers
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Baseus at 888-568-7216 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email care@baseus.com, or visit https://www.baseus.com/pages/recall_select_BS-30KP365 for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Baseus portable charger in black
Recalled Baseus portable charger in white
Recalled Baseus portable charger with SN ending in the letter "D" (label on bottom)
Recalled Baseus portable charger with SN ending in Arabic numerals (label on bottom)

Injuries.Name: Baseus has received 76 reports of incidents involving the portable chargers, including 72 reports of bulging and four reports of fire, including three reports of property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, AliExpress.com, Walmart.com, and Baseus.com from April 2020 to April 2025, priced between $39 and $90.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Baseus Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the charger and contact Baseus for a free replacement with a different serial number.

recalls.metaDescription: Baseus recalls 65W portable chargers due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: AliExpress has issued a recall for RGB-branded LED strip lights that are powered by button batteries. These color-changing lights, measuring approximately 0.4 inches, were available in various colors including white, warm white, blue, ice blue, green, red, yellow, purple, and pink. The design allows easy access to the lithium coin batteries, posing a serious ingestion hazard for children.
recalls.Title: AliExpress Recalls RGB LED Strip Lights Due to Ingestion Risk and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to AliExpress through their online service at https://helpcenter.aliexpress.com/s/BuyerHelp/home?language=en_US&m_station=BuyerHelp, email us_product_recall@aliexpress.com, or visit https://helpcenter.aliexpress.com/s/BuyerHelp/knowledge?categoryId=1000110454&language=en_US&m_station=BuyerHelp&questionId=1061297473&regulation=US-product-recall for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled AliExpress LED strip light - purple
Recalled AliExpress LED strip lights
Recalled AliExpress LED strip light - green

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available on AliExpress.com from March 2024 to November 2024 for approximately $3.

Distributors.Name: Dongguan Changsheng Heng Electronic Commerce Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: The LED strip lights present a significant ingestion hazard due to easy access to lithium coin batteries, violating federal safety regulations. Swallowing these batteries can lead to severe injuries, chemical burns, and even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled LED strip lights immediately and contact AliExpress for a full refund. They should destroy the lights by cutting them in half and disposing of them properly. A photo of the destroyed lights must be sent to us_product_recall@aliexpress.com or in response to the recall email from AliExpress. Note: Proper disposal of button cell and coin batteries should follow local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.metaDescription: AliExpress recalls RGB LED strip lights due to ingestion hazards. Immediate action required for refund and safe disposal.

recalls.Description: Casely is recalling its Power Pods 5000mAh portable MagSafe wireless phone chargers, identified by model number E33A. These compact chargers, measuring 3 x 2 x 0.25 inches and weighing approximately 3.2 ounces, were available in various colors and prints. The brand name 'Casely' is engraved on the front, while the model number is printed on the back. Consumers should be aware that improper disposal of these lithium-ion batteries can lead to fire hazards. They are advised not to discard these devices in regular trash or recycling bins, but to check with local hazardous waste centers for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: Casely Recalls Power Pods Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Casely at 888-964-9331 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at recall@getcasely.com, or online at https://www.getcasely.com/pages/2025-recall. More information can also be found by visiting www.getcasely.com and navigating to the 'Recalls' section at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Casely wireless portable power bank
Recalled Casely wireless portable power bank (back of pod)
Recalled Casely wireless portable power bank (model number)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 51 consumer reports of the lithium-ion battery overheating, expanding or catching fire while they were charging their phones, resulting in six minor burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available online at getcasely.com, Amazon.com, and other e-commerce platforms from March 2022 through September 2024, priced between $30 and $70.

Importers.Name: Casely Inc., of Brookyln, New York

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact Casely for a free replacement. Consumers must fill out an online form and submit two photos: one showing the front of the power bank with 'Recalled' and the date written in permanent marker, and another displaying the model number on the back. Dispose of the power banks according to local regulations. Proof of purchase is not necessary.

recalls.metaDescription: Casely recalls Power Pods due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free replacement.
